title: Auxtho Core Overview
description: Auxtho Core is the internal architecture used to control and verify how AI-generated plans move toward execution.
public_note: This page documents core architectural concepts of Auxtho Core as of 2025. Detailed implementations and proprietary components are not publicly disclosed.
intro: Auxtho Core is the internal architecture used to control and verify how AI-generated work moves toward execution.

The goal is not to let AI produce a result and hope that later review will catch problems. The goal is to ensure that execution passes through a defined verification path before it is allowed to proceed.
In practice, this means the system separates intent, planning, verification, and execution into distinct stages so that a plan can be assessed before it becomes an action.

core_flow.label: Input
core_flow.detail: A request enters the system with user context and operating constraints.

core_flow.label: Intent
core_flow.detail: The requested outcome is interpreted into a bounded intent that can be checked.

core_flow.label: Plan
core_flow.detail: The system produces an execution plan rather than acting immediately.

core_flow.label: Verification
core_flow.detail: The plan is checked against user intent and control criteria before execution.

core_flow.label: Execution
core_flow.detail: Only verified plans are allowed to proceed to the execution path.

core_flow.label: Output
core_flow.detail: The resulting output remains tied to the verified path that produced it.

isp: ISP, or Intent Synchronization Protocol, is the control protocol used to determine whether an AI-generated execution plan remains aligned with what the user actually intended.
The protocol is designed to intercept planning before execution. A plan that does not satisfy the defined intent criteria is not treated as executable simply because it was generated successfully.
The point is not execution after review. The point is verification before execution.
execution_control: AI does not hold direct execution authority.
AI generates a plan, while a separate verification layer evaluates that plan.
Execution is permitted only when the plan passes the verification criteria.
System behavior is therefore determined by the verification result rather than by generation alone.
policy_structure: Behavior criteria are managed as policy data rather than being embedded only as hard-coded assumptions.
This allows versioning, controlled change history, and later review of what standard was active at the time of execution.
The intended direction is a verification structure that remains reproducible under the same conditions.

timeline.event: ISP defined as an internal control concept for intent-aligned execution.
Auxtho Core designed as the architecture that separates planning, verification, and execution.
Execution control and verification structure implemented as part of the governed workflow foundation.

note: This page describes architectural concepts and control intent. It does not publish detailed algorithms, code, or proprietary implementation structure.
